title: navarretia myersii subsp. myersii
intro: Navarretia myersii subsp. myersii, also known as Myers' pincushion or purple pincushion, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family. It is a wildflower native to California, known for its clusters of small, fragrant purple flowers. Traditionally, Navarretia myersii subsp. myersii has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe minor wounds and reduce swelling. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
